blockingIssues.description: The run executor only captures milestones after terminal finish/abort or after a non-terminal action has already executed, so the required initial starting page can be skipped entirely.
Multiple failure paths return a terminal result without forcing a final milestone capture, so fatal error and other terminal failure states can finish without the required evidence screenshot.
The integrated execute-run handler only generates self-reports for `result.ok` outcomes, so failed runs omit the required post-task self-report data.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that when the required wrangler port 8787 is already occupied by a pre-existing listener, workers may rely on passing automated integration tests plus `lsof`/`curl` diagnostics after recording the conflict in `.factory/library/environment.md` instead of killing the process.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `worker-entry-point` and `run-executor-integration` handoffs both encountered an existing `workerd` listener on 8787 and had to skip live wrangler verification; the environment note now exists, but the skill still assumes wrangler can always bind the mission-approved port.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that Worker features may require narrowly-scoped updates in shared workspace packages when the feature changes a cross-workspace protocol.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `self-report-gen` handoff had to update `packages/shared` and `packages/ai` so browser-executor completion payloads could carry keyed self-report answers and still run in the Worker runtime.

rejectedObservations.observation: Root `vitest.config.ts` still uses the deprecated `test.workspace` option.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected because this is a normal repository maintenance task, not a shared-state documentation or worker-guidance update.
